opportunities.suggested_topic: Review US tech overweight: current allocation is 20% against a 15% target.
Discuss Gulf real estate overweight: currently 15% vs IPS target of 10%.
Critical FX policy breach: SEK base-currency exposure is 35.5%, significantly below the 60% minimum.

weekend_changes.headline: Swedish Krona Struggles as Riksbank Maintains Cautious Stance, BBH Analysts Say
Saudi Arabia property market slows sharply as home sales drop 50 per cent in Q1
European ESG Bond & Loan Issuance Fell 24%

three_nbas.title: Execute plan to restore SEK 60% currency floor
three_nbas.rationale: The portfolio's SEK exposure is at 35.5%, a critical breach of the 60% IPS minimum. This requires selling or hedging approximately SEK 118M of non-SEK assets. This action is the top priority and directly addresses the client's primary concern.
three_nbas.projected_impact: Restores compliance with the IPS, reduces unhedged currency risk from USD and AED/SAR exposures, and sets the foundation for strategic reallocation.
three_nbas.computation_trace: SEK base share = Σ(mv where fx_exposure='SEK') / AUM = SEK 171,294,395 / SEK 481,843,043 = 35.55%. Required remediation = AUM * (Floor - Current) = 481.84M * (0.60 - 0.3555) = SEK 117.82M.

three_nbas.title: Trim overweight Gulf RE & US Tech sleeves
three_nbas.rationale: Both sleeves are 5.0pp overweight. Recent market intel contradicts the client's 'still well bid' assumption for Gulf RE (Gulf REIT index -3.9% MoM, Saudi sales -50% Q1). This provides a tactical window to trim the 15% allocation back towards the 10% target, funding part of the FX remediation. The US tech trim aligns with the client's stated valuation discomfort.
three_nbas.projected_impact: Reduces concentration risk, crystallizes gains, and generates ~SEK 48M in proceeds for reallocation to underweight sleeves (EU Fixed Income) and restoring SEK exposure.
three_nbas.computation_trace: Drift = current_alloc − target_alloc. Gulf real estate: 15.0% − 10.0% = +5.00pp. US tech: 20.0% − 15.0% = +5.00pp. Proceeds from trimming 5% of AUM from each sleeve would be SEK ~24.09M each.

three_nbas.title: Remediate single-position limit breaches
three_nbas.rationale: Eight positions have breached the 5% single-position limit, including client-mentioned Hexagon (5.27%). The most significant are Emirates REIT (8.3%) and Investor AB (7.6%). This is a direct IPS violation requiring a systematic trim plan to restore compliance.
three_nbas.projected_impact: Reduces idiosyncratic risk from oversized single positions and ensures adherence to the portfolio's risk management framework as defined in the IPS.
three_nbas.computation_trace: Single-name position > 5% AUM check. Breaches confirmed for: Emirates REIT (8.30%), Investor AB (7.57%), Atlas Copco (6.97%), Volvo AB (5.83%), German Bund (5.81%), Jabal Omar (6.71%), Hexagon AB (5.27%), Assa Abloy (5.11%).

risk_flags.note: SEK base-currency exposure is 35.5%, a critical breach of the 60% IPS minimum. A remediation of SEK ~118M is required to restore compliance.
Multiple single-name positions have breached the 5% AUM limit. Key breaches include Emirates REIT (8.3%), Investor AB (7.6%), Atlas Copco (7.0%), and Hexagon (5.3%).
